steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Rewrite $2x - 6y > 12$ as $x - 3y > 6$. A region is NOT in the solution set if no point in it can satisfy this inequality. Test each choice by checking the sign of $2x - 6y$ in that region.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$x < 0$ and $y > 0$': $2x < 0$ and $-6y < 0$, so $2x - 6y < 0 < 12$. The inequality can never be satisfied here. ✓
  • Choice B — '$x < 0$ and $y < 0$': $-6y > 0$, so $2x - 6y$ can be large and positive (e.g. $x = -1, y = -100$ gives $598 > 12$). ✗
  • Choice C — '$x > 0$ and $y > 0$': Large $x$ with small $y$ satisfies the inequality (e.g. $x = 10, y = 1$ gives $14 > 12$). ✗
  • Choice D — 'the $x$-axis': On the $x$-axis $y = 0$, so the inequality becomes $2x > 12 \ Rightarrow x > 6$, which many points satisfy. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
